steps.action: Diff content.opf manifest sections for original 6 books (Martyr, ThreeBelow, Elvis, Familiars, HungerGames, TOG)
steps.observed: All 6 books produce 0 diffs in content.opf overall (394/394 text files match)
content.opf not in diff list — 0 diffs
content.opf matches perfectly
content.opf matches perfectly

Manifest entries structurally identical but differ in filenames (section: '1.xhtml' vs 'UYqzWVgySW_Gl4WQ-Od_xQ1.xhtml'; resource: 'resource_7-resized-1073-1621.bin' vs 'image_7-resized-1073-1621.jpg') — this is a known filename convention gap (VAL-EPUB-002 / VAL-RES-001), NOT a manifest structure issue. Media-types, properties, and ordering all match correctly.

steps.expected: Go code sets cover-image property on cover resource
steps.observed: yj_to_epub_resources.go:672 sets output[index].Properties = "cover-image"
kfx_test.go:284 and kfx_test.go:602 both assert properties="cover-image" in content.opf

steps.expected: Python EPUB has properties="cover-image" on cover item
steps.observed: SecretsCrown Python content.opf: image_7-resized-1073-1621.jpg has properties="cover-image"

steps.expected: Go EPUB has properties="cover-image" on cover item
steps.observed: SecretsCrown Go content.opf: Missing image resources in manifest (Go omits image entries due to resource naming convention gap), but cover-image property is correctly set in yj_to_epub_resources.go

content.opf matches perfectly

steps.action: Verify guide entries in SecretsCrown Go vs Python
steps.observed: Both have 3 guide entries: cover (Cover), toc (Table of Contents), text (Beginning) — types and titles match. Hrefs differ only due to filename convention gap.

steps.observed: internal/epub/epub.go:483: `len(book.Guide) > 0 && (generateEpub2 || book.GenerateEpub2Compatible)`

steps.expected: Guide types map correctly to epub types in nav
steps.observed: yj_to_epub_navigation.go:679-688 maps guide types (cover→cover, toc→toc, text→bodymatter)

steps.action: Grep for dc:creator in Go epub code
steps.expected: Go generates dc:creator with id and refines
steps.observed: internal/epub/epub.go:372: `<dc:creator id="creatorN">Author</dc:creator>` with meta refines for role
internal/epub/epub.go:373: `<meta refines="#creatorN" property="role" scheme="marc:relators">aut</meta>`
internal/epub/epub.go:379: `<dc:creator opf:role="aut">`
Go: creator0=Jacobson, Andrew + creator1=Epstein, Adam Jay — both with marc:relators aut refines. Matches Python exactly.
kfx_test.go:597-598 asserts dc:creator with marc:relators refines

steps.action: Grep for marc in Go code
steps.observed: internal/epub/epub.go:355: EPUB3 package element includes `prefix="marc: http://id.loc.gov/vocabulary/"`

steps.action: Verify marc:relators usage in creator refinements
steps.observed: internal/epub/epub.go:373: `scheme="marc:relators"` in meta refines element
Go: `prefix="marc: http://id.loc.gov/vocabulary/"` and `scheme="marc:relators"` — matches Python exactly
kfx_test.go:595 asserts `prefix="marc: http://id.loc.gov/vocabulary/"`

summary: Tested 8 assertions: all 8 passed. Original 6 books (394/394) have 0 content.opf diffs. New books 1984, HeatedRivalry, SunriseReaping also have 0 content.opf diffs. SecretsCrown has content.opf diffs but they are entirely caused by upstream filename convention gaps (section filenames: '1.xhtml' vs hash-based; resource filenames: 'resource_N.bin' vs 'image_N.jpg') — NOT by metadata, manifest structure, spine, guide, or creator/marc handling. All metadata fields (dc:identifier/ASIN, dc:title, dc:creator with marc:relators refines, dc:language, dc:publisher, dc:date, cover meta, guide entries, spine toc) match perfectly between Go and Python across all 10 books.
